response.text:
# REFLECTION

r1c4 is in a critical energy deficit while islanded, with SoC at 33% (1 kWh usable of 3 kWh capacity) and a 1 kW load consuming the battery at ~1 hour to depletion. The memory log shows a consistent pattern: multiple peers (r0c4, r1c3, r1c5, r2c4) have been requesting energy over the past 90 minutes, but r1c4 has rejected all outbound transfers citing a conservative 50% SoC floor requirement for grid-reconnection readiness. However, this defensive posture is now counterproductive—the household is approaching critical depletion (33% SoC) while the grid remains down (islanded=True), and no inbound energy has materialized despite repeated rejections. The geographic round-robin fallback policy has failed to establish reciprocal trust or secure inbound support. At current consumption, r1c4 has ~20 minutes before SoC drops below 25%, at which point load-shedding becomes mandatory. The cluster appears to be in mutual deficit (r1c3 at 44%, r0c4 and r1c5 both requesting), suggesting no peer can reliably supply energy. The optimal strategy is to immediately shift to an urgent inbound-only posture, broadcast critical requests with high urgency, shed non-essential load to extend runway, and accept that the 50% SoC floor is unachievable without external supply—maintaining it will result in complete depletion.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.25
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded with 33% SoC and 1 kW load; ~20 min to critical depletion. Conservative 50% floor unachievable without inbound supply. Shift to inbound-only emergency posture. Shed load immediately. Broadcast urgent requests to all peers including r0c4, r1c3, r1c5, r2c4 despite prior rejections—cluster may have recovered or prioritize critical peer."
ttl_ticks: 5
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
